title: Gone girls | Death of 14 Juang girls from Odisha in Tiruvallur ammonia gas leak exposes tribe’s socio-economic struggle
content: The death of 14 girls from the Juang community, a Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Group, from Odisha at a seafood processing plant in Tamil Nadu’s Tiruvallur has brought to the fore the socio-economic realities of marginalised tribal populations despite decades of targeted interventions. Satyasundar Barik visits the Juang villagers who lost their daughters to understand what went wrong in the development journey

title: Karur visit LIVE: CM Vijay arrives at Tiruchi Airport by special flight, en route to Karur
content: Extensive security arrangements have been put in place across Karur for the Chief Minister's visit, with barricades, QR code-enabled entry passes for participants and heavy police deployment at all venues.

title: Not a Modi breakthrough, Australia's ruling party approved uranium sales to India in 2011: Congress
content: The Opposition party's assertion came after BJP IT department head Amit Malviya said that in 2010, Australia refused to sell uranium to India, but under Modi's leadership, the two countries have struck a uranium export agreement.

title: All private schools in T.N. must display fee structure on notice boards, websites: Madras High Court
content: Justice M. Dhandapani issues the direction by exercising the extraordinary writ jurisdiction of the High Court under Article 226 of the Constitution

title: Air India flight from Thiruvananthapuram to Bengaluru aborts takeoff after technical snag
content: Airport officials say the passengers are still inside the flight. Air India will be arranging another flight to Bengaluru to accommodate them

title: CM Suvendu Adhikari says central hospital monitoring system will help get rid of ‘touts’
content: CM and State health officials review progress of central hospital monitoring system at the Health Department headquarters

title: What a dolphin’s death in Kerala reveals about India’s marine plastic pollution
content: Necropsy of an endangered Indian Ocean humpback dolphin that washed ashore on Kozhikode beach recently reveals fatal plastic fishing net ingestion, highlighting growing threats from marine pollution and ‘ghost nets’. CMFRI data show marine mammal strandings remain high, with coastal dolphins among the species most vulnerable

title: Over 56,000 in Karnataka unaware of HIV status; dating apps pose new challenge to tracing infections
content: While an estimated 56,000 people in Karnataka are living with HIV unknowingly, over 2 lakh are on treatment, posing a major challenge to the State’s HIV control programme

title: 7,948 Assam schools without ideal pupil-teacher ratio
content: Education Minister Ranoj Pegu had earlier said that there are 2,670 single-teacher government primary schools

title: Confusion over errors in filling enumeration forms
content: While some made errors owing to lack of awareness, some were dealing with problems due to incorrect spellings in 2002 rolls; call for instructional video to fill the form in Hindi, Urdu, English

title: Exploring monsoon trails in the Eastern Ghats
content: As mist, rain and fresh greenery transform the Eastern Ghats, guided treks are drawing adventurers to summit trails, waterfalls and remote hill villages

title: PM Modi meets Australia's Opposition leader, discusses bilateral ties
content: The Prime Minister landed in Australia on July 8 after completing his visit to Indonesia. From here, he is scheduled to leave for New Zealand — the last leg of his six-day, three-nation tour.

title: Why is Gujarat betting  ₹6 lakh crore on data centres? | Explained
content: The State hopes to attract investments worth ₹6 lakh crore and build 7.5 GW of data centre capacity over the next few years, positioning itself alongside established hubs such as Maharashtra, Tamil Nadu and Telangana

title: Jana Nayagan gets ‘A’ certificate; likely to release in theatres on July 24
content: According to reports, after the makers implemented modifications suggested by the examining committee, the film was initially considered suitable for a 'U/A' certificate; however, the process stalled when a committee member raised an internal objection, claiming certain portions could hurt public sentiments.

title: Prambanan Temple and Indonesia’s journey with Hinduism | Explained
content: The Prambanan Temple is significant in Indonesia’s Hinduism, highlighting its evolution under state-mandated monotheism and religious freedom

title: Four shooters killed in Gurugram encounter after attack on businessman’s house
content: In the exchange of fire, all five shooters were hit. They were rushed to hospital where four were declared dead. One is undergoing treatment.

title: Who is to mind the creche in Karnataka?
content: The Karnataka government issued guidelines for establishing crèche/daycare centres back in 2015. The alleged child abuse incident in a centre for Capgemini employees in Bengaluru reveals that these have remained on paper, with even a nodal agency not named to monitor them, write Shilpa Elizabeth and Rashmi Patil

title: When killer waves snuffed out six lives in Visakhapatnam
content: Braving high seas and rough weather, fishermen along the Visakhapatnam coast weather many a storm to eke out a living. But this time, the bad weather and the high seas have swallowed six fishermen, leaving their families in dire straits, writes V. Kamalakara Rao

title: Redrawing Telangana’s cricket map
content: For years, the State’s cricket story was largely confined to Hyderabad. The inaugural TG20, its first franchise-based T20 league, is beginning to change that by offering district cricketers a platform to showcase their talent before fans, selectors and IPL scouts alike. Anirudh Velamuri explores how the tournament is opening new doors beyond the capital

title: Dwindling Fortunes: Quick-commerce platforms, large retailers threaten small businesses and neighbourhood shops in Kerala
content: Neighbourhood shops and small retailers across the country are finding the going tough with e-commerce platforms and corporate-backed large retail stores rapidly establishing their business even in rural areas. A walk along the commercial areas as well as neighbourhoods reveals the plight of those who have been struggling to cope in the digital era

title: Bankipur bypoll: voters say the battle will be between BJP and JSP
content: Jan Suraaj Party founder Prashant Kishor, who is in the fray, has been busy campaigning in the constituency; voters in the BJP stronghold say he will cut into the BJP’s votes

title: Four Indian nationals pushed into Bangladesh return home after one-year legal battle
content: The group, which included two minors, languished in Bangladesh for over a year before it returned home; it came back through the international border in Malda district

title: Prabhsimran credits Yuvraj, Ponting for his improvement
content: He has been picked for the three T20Is in Zimbabwe as the second wicket-keeper batter in the side after Ishan Kishan, with Sanju Samson surprisingly left out of the squad.

title: CISF seizes over 428 tonnes of illegal coal in Jharkhand, Bengal
content: In intensified crackdown, authorities take control of vehicles, equipment being used for illegal mining; 4 FIRs registered, legal action to be initiated

title: Supreme Court cites blazes in Delhi, Lucknow; questions civic bodies on unauthorised buildings
content: Bench says “slackness” by the civic authorities to take effective action against unauthorised constructions in the national capital and its neighbouring regions will not be tolerated

title: Supreme Court considers reference of provision to furnish reason during arrests
content: Top court cites conflicting rulings on whether arrest grounds must be furnished in writing while hearing Meghalaya’s appeal against Sonam Raghuvanshi’s bail

title: Assault on youths highlights growing vigilantism in Perumbavoor amid anti-drug crackdown
content: Three youths were reportedly publicly tried, assaulted, stripped, and forcibly tonsured by a mob that accused them of supplying ganja to a schoolboy. Six persons have been arrested in connection with the incident

title: Building tilt prompts Kochi Corpn. to seek mandatory licence
content: The move comes in the wake of a recent incident in which a two-storey building, while being lifted, tilted onto an adjacent commercial structure along M.G. Road

title: Panchayat in Kannur launches volunteer disaster response force
content: The initiative aims to ensure rapid rescue operations, reduce casualties, and strengthen community preparedness. The local body has also initiated a comprehensive safety literacy campaign

title: Street vendors’ organisations step up stir against Kannur Corporation’s ‘eviction drives’
content: The protesters demand the Corporation to halt eviction drives and implement the Street Vendors Act by surveying vendors and issuing identity cards to eligible traders

title: Greens demand immediate cancellation of Wayanad tunnel project
content: The protesters seek legal action against the contracting company, its blacklisting from future projects, and the filing of criminal charges against it

title: Heavy rain brings down 150-year-old clock tower at Kozhikode railway station
content: The collapse sent debris and roofing sheets crashing onto Platform No. 2 and the overhead traction wires. While no casualties were reported, train operations remained disrupted

title: Sudha Kongara suppressed receipt of ₹4.12 crore from 2D Entertainment for Parasakthi, Dawn Pictures tells Madras HC
content: Justice K. Kumaresh Babu reserves orders on the plea to restrain the release of Idhayam Murali after filing of a counter affidavit by the production house

title: Two former Ministers skip review meetings chaired by Palaniswami
content: P. Balakrishna Reddy of Hosur and K.P. Anbalagan of Palacode were absent at the review sessions held for Krishnagiri and Dharmapuri districts respectively

title: Report abnormal population growth in border areas: Shah to SPs
content: Union Home Minister Amit Shah says the government is committed to curb “unnatural demographic growth” caused by abnormal factors with a “ruthless approach”

title: Rescue mission enters final phase in Wayanad; three more bodies recovered
content: Two of the three victims whose bodies were recovered on Thursday have been identified as Rahul Sharma, an engineer from Himachal Pradesh, and Mohammad Imran, an excavator operator from Bihar.

title: Munnar to host ‘Rain 40' football tournament on August 9
content: Thirty-two teams will participate in the penalty shootout tournament — 16 from Idukki district and the rest from other districts

title: PM Modi in Australia updates: India, Australia seal landmark pacts on nuclear energy and defence
content: India and Australia decided to work expeditiously to firm up the proposed Comprehensive Economic Cooperation Agreement as well as a bilateral investment protection framework to further enhance the trade and investment relations.

title: Panel to be formed soon to examine liquor retail models in other States, says the Minister for Prohibition and Excise
content: The committee will study the functioning of excise departments in Kerala, Karnataka, Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, and Odisha, including liquor procurement, retail operations, and employee service conditions, says K. Vignesh

title: A missile launch, a nuclear message: Why China wanted the US to take notice
content: China recently launched a long-range missile from a submarine into the South Pacific. This action demonstrated Beijing's growing nuclear power and strategic reach. The test aimed to send a message primarily to the United States. Pacific nations expressed concern over the provocative and destabilizing act. This event highlights China's ongoing military modernization efforts.

title: Watch: Dejected Sooryavanshi walks off even before catch; reaction goes viral
content: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi faced disappointment after his dismissal in the fourth T20I match.  He scored low scores in his first three international matches.  Jofra Archer troubled the young batter with short-pitched bowling.  Sooryavanshi came with high expectations after a sensational IPL and U-19 World Cup.  India trails England three-nil in the ongoing five-match series.

title: New EPF Scheme 2026: 10 questions employees are asking about contributions, withdrawals & more
content: EPF Scheme 2026: The good news is that many core features of the provident fund system remain familiar. Existing PF accounts continue, contribution rates broadly remain unchanged and accumulated balances remain protected.

title: Google VP to Gemini users: We are fixing these 10 'problems' you have with Gemini app
content: Google VP Josh Woodward, who leads the Gemini app, asked users on X what the AI still couldn't do well. Over 1,700 replies poured in within hours. Woodward responded with a ranked top 10 list of complaints, from Workspace reliability to tool calling and chat organization, admitting Google should have fixed them sooner. But on one request, celebrity likeness guardrails, Google flatly refused to budge.

title: BJP councillor, 2 aides booked for assaulting Mahad council chief
content: An FIR was filed against a BJP councillor and two aides for assaulting an administrative officer. The assault occurred after the councillor questioned the non-resolution of a damaged water pipeline. The chief officer was reportedly hit with an umbrella and punched during the altercation. Council employees struck work demanding the immediate arrest of the accused individuals. Police have issued notices for cooperation in the ongoing investigation and court proceedings.

title: Principal calls student a thief, shows CCTV footage in class; court refuses to drop FIR
content: The Himachal Pradesh High Court refused to quash an FIR against a school principal. She was accused of calling a student a thief in front of classmates. A psychologist's report finding no trauma did not halt criminal proceedings. The court stated mental trauma needs proof during the trial stage. Allegations, if true, clearly amount to a crime, the court held.

title: Drummer, 19, found dead at hotel; kin blame girlfriend
content: A 19-year-old drummer was discovered deceased in a Bhilai hotel room on Wednesday. Preliminary evidence suggests suicide following an alcohol-fueled argument with his girlfriend. The victim's family, however, alleges murder and demands a thorough investigation into the incident. They claim the youth had multiple injury marks and questioned the girlfriend's actions. Police are verifying these claims as part of their ongoing examination.

title: Heat of Europe's AC ban comes to New York; Mayor Mamdani shares AC tips
content: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani urged residents to reduce electricity use.  He asked people to adjust AC settings and limit unnecessary power consumption.  The city is also implementing similar measures in government buildings.  Officials aim to reduce pressure on the power grid during the heat wave.  This appeal comes as electricity demand rises across New York City.

title: Watch: Morocco fans clash with police in London; Paris stays calm after France win
content: Morocco's World Cup journey concluded with a loss to France, leading to significant fan disturbances in London.  Riots erupted on Edgware Road, with police confronting crowds throwing projectiles.  Paris experienced largely peaceful celebrations despite initial concerns about potential unrest.  Earlier, Moroccan supporters clashed with police in The Hague after a Round-of-16 victory.

title: Billionaire Stephen Schwarzman’s $400M superyacht is packed with luxuries
content: Stephen Schwarzman, the chairman of Blackstone, has made headlines by purchasing a lavish superyacht named Destiny. This stunning 101.2-meter craft, which set him back around $400 million, comes from the renowned builder Feadship. Among its luxurious features are a glass-bottom pool and a helipad. Notably, Schwarzman started his remarkable career with a modest $400,000 investment, and he is often seen sporting affordable Swatch watches.

title: Scientists thickened Arctic ice with seawater, but there is a big catch
content: Scientists tested spraying seawater onto Arctic ice, which showed promising initial results. The treated ice became thicker and more reflective, resisting summer melting effectively. This method offers an alternative to controversial geoengineering approaches for ice preservation. While promising, researchers note significant challenges for large-scale implementation across the Arctic. Further work is essential before this technique can be considered for broader application.

title: Melinda Gates to students: When a woman steps into power, it is better for all
content: Melinda French Gates received the Josef Korbel Award for her philanthropic work. She stressed that empowering women builds stronger families and nations. French Gates highlighted the importance of centering women in philanthropic efforts. Her organization Pivotal invests in entrepreneurs and advocates for paid leave. She urged students to contribute their skills to local communities.

title: Watch: Massive wildfire turns sky orange in southern Spain as death toll rises to 12
content: A catastrophic wildfire ravaging southern Spain has sadly resulted in the loss of twelve lives, with numerous others suffering injuries due to the rapidly spreading flames. The extreme heat and arid conditions exacerbated the situation, hindering firefighting efforts. This event stands as one of the most tragic wildfires in Spain's recent history. Investigations into the cause of the fire are currently underway.

title: Lord's set for maiden women's Test match as England host India in historic clash
content: This Friday, Lord's Cricket Ground will make history by hosting its first-ever women's Test match, featuring a thrilling four-day contest between England and India. This significant event marks a pivotal moment, as it comes more than fifty years after the venue's first women’s international match. Ticket sales have soared, with over thirty thousand already sold. Unfortunately, India's opener Pratika Rawal will miss the match due to a knee injury.

title: How an extortion bid ended in gun battle that killed 4 gangsters in Gurgaon
content: Four alleged shooters linked to gangster Deepak Nandal were killed in a fierce gun battle.  The encounter occurred after the suspects targeted a businessman's residence with extortion demands.  Police retaliated in self-defense after the accused opened fire and attempted to escape.  Three police personnel sustained bullet injuries during the intense exchange of gunfire.  Investigators recovered five pistols, including three foreign-made weapons, from the encounter site.

title: Despite all-Argentinian referee row, France extend astonishing World Cup record vs Morocco
content: France defeated Morocco 2-0 in a World Cup quarter-final match. This victory extended France's record of winning matches officiated by Argentinian referees. The appointment of an all-Argentinian refereeing team had sparked controversy before the game. Despite the debate, France secured their win and advanced to the next stage. FIFA defended the integrity and independence of its match officials.

title: Karnataka UUCMS results 2026 for UG, PG courses declared; here is how to check
content: The Department of Higher Education, Government of Karnataka, has announced the UUCMS 2026 semester results for undergraduate and postgraduate courses across participating state universities. Students can download their provisional digital marks cards by logging into the unified portal with their enrolment number and password. The platform displays internal assessment and theory examination marks together. Authorities have also provided helpline and email support for login issues, data discrepancies and revaluation-related technical problems.

title: FIFA makes major VAR change for remainder of World Cup after teams raise complaints
content: FIFA has changed its VAR operations for the remainder of the 2026 World Cup, placing backup VAR officials inside stadiums after growing criticism over refereeing decisions during the knockout stages. The new system keeps Dallas as the central hub while adding on-site support to prevent technical disruptions. FIFA insists the move aims to improve reliability, not influence match decisions.

title: Deleted chats & calls to girlfriend: How US cops caught Telangana techie for wife's murder
content: A husband faces murder charges in Washington after his wife's death was initially ruled sudden. Autopsy revealed Raajitha Sabbineni died from strangulation, leading to Avinash Narne's arrest. Investigators allege Narne had an affair and contacted another woman on the day of the incident. He reportedly sent her a photograph of his deceased wife's body. The investigation continues as community members express shock at the allegations.

title: Rare anaesthetic complication linked to man's death explained
content: A British teacher died after a hair transplant procedure in Turkey.  He developed convulsions and cardiac arrest following local anaesthetic administration.  The coroner suggested Local Anaesthetic Systemic Toxicity as a possible cause.  This rare condition can affect the brain and heart, becoming life-threatening.  Experts urge careful consideration of all medical procedure risks.

title: Taylor Swift-Blake Lively friendship faces fresh scrutiny after report
content: Reports have renewed speculation about Taylor Swift and Blake Lively's friendship after Lively and Ryan Reynolds were reportedly absent from Swift's wedding celebrations. Anonymous sources claimed Lively was disappointed and hoped to repair the friendship, but no invitation came. Other reports suggested the relationship may be beyond repair. However, neither Swift nor Lively has publicly addressed the rumors, leaving the claims based solely on unnamed insider reports.

title: Kim Kardashian reportedly stepped back from Lewis Hamilton romance
content: Lewis Hamilton and the billionaire reality TV star and popular entrepreneur Kim Kardashian have turned heads with their whirlwind romance, sparking chaos among many. While there have been many speculations about Kim Kardashian and Lewis Hamilton heading for a break up, it seems the socialite has given this relationship a lot of thought.

title: How to get Holofoil Sprite in Fortnite: Location, ability, and more
content: Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 3 introduces Holofoil Sprites which boost rare item discovery chances. These companions offer a five percent increase to finding rarer Sprite variants. Players can find these Sprites near Golden Fishing Spots and specific map locations. A special Holofoil Hours event on July 11, 2026, will increase spawn rates. This event also provides players with a Self-Revive Device and Shock Rock effects.

title: Child sick after eating expired instant noodles; consumer court orders Rs 20,000 in relief
content: A consumer commission ordered a retailer to refund expired noodles and pay compensation. The minor daughter fell ill after consuming the product, which had passed its expiry date. The commission rejected the seller's 'buyer beware' defense, emphasizing seller responsibility. It ruled the retailer solely liable for failing to remove old stock. The family received a refund, compensation, and litigation costs.

title: Explained: Why Mbappe's penalty was delayed by more than three minutes against Morocco
content: Kylian Mbappe's penalty was delayed for over three minutes by a VAR review.  The France captain's initial penalty attempt was then comfortably saved by the goalkeeper.  Despite the missed opportunity, Mbappe scored France's opening goal in the second half.  He also assisted Ousmane Dembele for the second goal in their victory.  This win secured France's place in the World Cup semifinals for a third consecutive time.

title: 'Cricket a shared passion': PM Modi visits Australia's Melbourne Cricket Ground
content: "Stepping into the MCG evokes two simultaneous emotions for any Indian. First, the thrill of an India-Australia match and second, the realisation that, in both our nations, cricket is not merely a sport but a shared passion. Yet, today, there is no pressure of a 'last-over finish.' Instead, there is only the joy of the game, the warmth of our friendship, and the energy of future champions," PM Modi said. ​​

title: Top shares to buy: Stock recommendations for today - check list
content: Stock market recommendations: Bharti Airtel, and HDFC Bank - these are the top 2 stocks that Bajaj Broking Research has recommended for buying on July 10, 2026:

title: Only 4 players in 60 years! Mbappe joins Messi with rare FIFA World Cup feat
content: Kylian Mbappe achieved a rare FIFA World Cup feat by scoring and assisting against Morocco. He joined Lionel Messi in an exclusive club after a remarkable all-round performance. Mbappe recovered from a missed penalty to score his eighth goal of the tournament. He also provided an assist for Ousmane Dembele's crucial second goal. France advanced to the semifinals, continuing their title defense in the competition.

title: Are schools closed in Telangana today? Private schools suspend classes amid bandh
content: Many private and corporate schools across Telangana are closed today, July 10, 2026, because of a statewide bandh organised by student groups, while the state government has not declared an official holiday. Government schools have received no closure notification, although protests may affect normal operations. The bandh has been called over school consolidation plans, fee regulation, and vacancies in the education department. Parents have been advised to verify school operations directly with individual institutions.

title: Which countries have F-35, why Turkey lost US access & why Israel is not happy - explained
content: Turkey's F-35 fighter jet access is under review by the US administration. This potential return follows Turkey's purchase of Russian S-400 air defense systems. Israel opposes this move, citing regional military balance concerns. The US faces challenges navigating legal and congressional restrictions on this decision. The F-35 program involves many allied nations for collective security.

title: Mbappé vs Messi: Why France star leads Golden Boot race despite being level on goals
content: Kylian Mbappé has moved ahead of Lionel Messi in the FIFA World Cup 2026 Golden Boot race despite both stars being level on eight goals. FIFA’s tiebreaker rules favour Mbappé due to his three assists compared to Messi’s one. With Erling Haaland and Harry Kane still in contention, the battle for the tournament’s top scorer remains wide open.

title: 'Happy with my performance, but...': Shreyas Iyer reflects on defeat to England
content: England secured the T20I series with a commanding nine-wicket victory over India.  Captain Shreyas Iyer acknowledged the team fell short in all aspects of the game.  India posted 158/7 after recovering from an early top-order collapse.  England chased down the target quickly thanks to a strong partnership.  Iyer expressed disappointment despite his personal unbeaten knock.

title: Crude dips but eyes weekly gain as US-Iran conflict stokes supply fears
content: Oil prices saw a slight decrease but are set for significant weekly gains. Renewed military exchanges between the United States and Iran kept crude supply concerns elevated. The conflict has delayed the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, a critical energy chokepoint. Worries over slowing global demand and inflation are limiting sharper price rallies. Producer price inflation in China surged, increasing pressure on manufacturers.

title: AIBE 21 final answer key 2026 to release today; here's how to check results
content: AIBE 21 final answer key 2026: The Bar Council of India (BCI) is scheduled to release the AIBE 21 Final Answer Key 2026 on July 10, 2026, following the completion of the objection review process. The AIBE 21 results are expected between July 15 and July 18. Candidates can download the revised answer key from the official portal and check qualifying marks. The BCI has also outlined the Certificate of Practice issuance process, including digital verification, physical distribution and document collection guidelines.

title: Kylian Mbappe makes unwanted World Cup history with first-ever ...
content: Kylian Mbappé missed the first World Cup penalty of his career during France's 2-0 quarter-final win over Morocco, becoming only the second French player to fail from the spot in regulation time at the tournament. After a lengthy VAR review and Yassine Bounou's save, Mbappé bounced back with a stunning second-half goal to send France into the semi-finals.

title: Iran's Ali Khamenei buried in Mashhad, successor son Mojtaba still missing
content: Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was buried in Mashhad after a week of ceremonies. He ruled Iran for nearly thirty-seven years and died in recent airstrikes. Large crowds gathered for funeral processions which brought daily life to a standstill. His eldest son led prayers, but his successor was notably absent from the burial. This burial occurred amid renewed hostilities and recent strikes between the US and Iran.

title: Nithin Kamath's Swipe, Groww's Reply: India's Top Brokers Clash On X
content: Zerodha's CEO Nithin Kamath came days after reports of Groww starting to offering regular mutual funds under its new Groww Prime offering.

title: Skipping Motor Insurance Renewal? Here's How It Can Cost You More
content: The biggest casualty of a delayed renewal is 'No Claim Bonus'. A lapsed policy can also trigger a vehicle inspection before a fresh policy is issued.

title: Concrete Jungles, Clogged Drains: Decoding India's Waterlogging Crisis
content: The recurring incidents of waterlogging raise serious questions about the country's civic strengths and weaknesses, a lot of which stand exposed as India experiences the monsoon.

title: Video: Thai PM Plays Saxophone, Malaysian PM Performs "My Way" During Official Lunch
content: The performance took place during Charnvirakul Anutin's two-day official visit to Malaysia, where he held bilateral talks on ways to strengthen ties between their countries.

title: Attacks On Salman Khan, Siddhu Moosewala: Inside US' Charges Against Lawrence Bishnoi
content: The US probe found that Bishnoi continued directing his associates across the globe personally using contraband cellphones and other voice-over-internet-protocol devices smuggled into his jail cell.

title: Stock Market LIVE Updates, Sensex Today: Sensex Jumps 800 Points Higher, Asian Markets Advance
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Brent crude traded near $76 while US West Texas Intermediate was below $72 per barrel.

title: Bullying, Call For Help: CCTV Shows Moments Before Jaipur Schoolgirl's Death
content: Amaira's family has alleged inaction on the part of the police and has called for stricter provisions of the Juvenile Justice (JJ) Act to be applied, not only against the class teacher but also against the school's principal and chairman.

title: "US Bases Our Problem, Not Neighbours": Iranian Experts On Middle East Attacks
content: Professor Seyed Emamain, Founder of Governance and Policy Thinktank (GPTT), and Ali Salehian, Head of the International Department GPTT, spoke to NDTV about the mass uprising against Ali Khamenei's rule in January to mass support after his death.

title: Weather LIVE Updates: Landslide Alert In Himachal Pradesh
content: Weather Today, Delhi-NCR Rain, Monsoon Live Updates: Southwest Monsoon causes heavy rain and waterlogging in Delhi NCR and Uttar Pradesh

title: 4 Gangsters Killed, 3 Cops Injured, 60 Rounds Of Firing In Gurugram Encounter
content: During the exchange of fire, all five alleged shooters sustained bullet injuries. They were shifted to a private super-specialty hospital, where doctors declared four of them dead.

title: Explosions Heard In Iran As Ali Khamenei Is Buried, US Denies Strikes
content: A US-Israeli projectile reportedly hit a military headquarters on the outskirts of Bushehr, home to Tehran's only civilian nuclear plant, on Thursday.

title: "Trauma Of Giving Birth In Jail Not Bearable": Court On Giving Bail To TCS Staffer Nida Khan
content: Nida Khan, who has been named in the sexual harassment and religious conversion cases at TCS's unit in Maharashtra's Nashik, is five months pregnant.

title: One QR Scan Can Tell If Your Medicine Is Fake. Here's How It Works
content: The government has steadily expanded the use of QR codes to make fake medicines harder to introduce into the supply chain.

title: Andy Burnham Set To Become Next UK PM As 80% Labour MPs Back Him
content: Andy Burnham is on the brink of becoming Britain's next prime minister after securing the backing on Thursday from 80% of Labour lawmakers in a party leadership contest.

title: OpenAI's Number 2 Executive Fidji Simo Steps Down To Focus On health
content: Top Silicon Valley executive Fidji Simo announced Wednesday she is leaving her full-time role at OpenAI to go part-time as an advisor and focus on recovery from a chronic illness.

title: Ex-Epoch Times Finance Head Pleads Guilty In $67 Million Fraud
content: Judge Victor Marrero did not immediately set a sentencing date for Weidong "Bill" Guan, 63, who remains free on bail.

title: Charlie Kirk Killing: Roommate Says Suspect "Wishes He Hadn't Done It"
content: The man accused of murdering influential right-wing campaigner Charlie Kirk broke down in tears of regret days after the killing, according to video testimony played in a US court on Thursday.

title: 8 Indicted For Plotting Drone, Sniper Attack On White House UFC Event
content: According to the new indictment, the plot began in May, when the group began amassing money, firearms, ammunition, body armor, explosives, drones, medical equipment, communications equipment and other items.

content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock.
